title: Scientism : Romanticism : Psychotherapy - and Buddhadhamma.To play the media you will need to either update your browser to a recent version or update yourFlash pluginThere is a lot on the web if you google - scientism.Some books I found especially useful:Scientism. A Word we Need By John Crowburn SJ. Especially useful to people like me who know virtually nothing about science. Though pitched towards Roman Catholics, I did not find it biased when compared to other books I read.Yval N.Hararitwo books:SapiensandHomo Deusretell the whole human story and where we may be heading. Fascinating and again for the general reader. It through this work I finally under stood this mysterious word - algorithm.Romanticism by Thanissaro Bhikkhuis
justification: This talk directly and systematically addresses the chapter theme by examining three major Western intellectual frameworks—scientism, romanticism, and psychotherapy—and how each filters or distorts the Buddha's teaching. It offers the most comprehensive comparative analysis, with clear definitions, historical context, philosophical depth, and practical examples. The structure naturally serves as a spine onto which complementary material can be grafted, and Bhante's voice comes through with characteristic clarity and accessible scholarship.
summary: Bhante Bodhidhamma examines how Buddhism is received through three Western cultural lenses: scientific materialism, romantic idealism, and psychotherapy. He clarifies what the Buddha actually taught about consciousness, ethics, and transcendence, distinguishing authentic Dhamma from Western interpretive distortions.

relevance.note: Directly examines Buddhism through Western cultural lenses
